description: We register the ownership of land and property in England and Wales. HM Land Registry is a non-ministerial department.

details.body:
We register the ownership of land and property in England and Wales.

HM Land Registry is a non-ministerial department.

details.body:
The Chief Financial Officer is responsible for the financial management of HM Land Registry and compliance with the general requirements of Government Accounting.

They oversee the preparation of our annual accounts and are responsible for ensuring the key performance indicator for efficiency is achieved. They provide advice to the Chief Executive and the Portfolio Board on the regularity and propriety of expenditure and also provide advice on commercial contracts.

The Chief Land Registrar is responsible for keeping the land registers and has all the powers, responsibilities and duties conferred and imposed by law in relation to that.

The Chief Land Registrar is also HM Land Registry’s Chief Executive and Principal Accounting Officer. This role is responsible for the efficient and effective day-to-day management of the organisation, including overseeing its objectives and the implementation of an approved business strategy and business plan.

details.body: The Director of Finance, was responsible for ensuring that the Finance and Procurement group support the Executive Committee and UKTI Board in delivering their corporate functions through securing, managing and overseeing resources.
The interim role has responsibility for corporate strategy, external engagement, customer relations, service design and product management, policy development and enterprise design.

details.body:
The HR Director is responsible for setting and delivering HM Land Registry’s people strategy. This includes strategic workforce planning, leadership and management development, employee relations, wellbeing, learning and development, and ensuring colleagues have the tools and support they need to perform effectively. 

The role also supports the organisation’s strategic objectives by embedding inclusive behaviours and effective performance management across HM Land Registry.

role_appointments.title: Emily Hobbs - Director of Human Resources

details.body:
The director is responsible for:

  • overseeing the organisation’s transformational ambition and the programmes to deliver on this
  • development, operation and maintenance of new and existing technology solutions and systems
  • working across the organisation and sector to deliver service improvements for both customers and staff
  • ensuring the organisation’s technology is resilient and effectively underpins HMLR’s business operations
  • collaborating with and supporting colleagues to successfully embed changes

role_appointments.title: Mark Gray - Chief Transformation & Technology Officer

details.body: The role is accountable for driving service excellence and operational performance in the delivery of our Land Registration services over the medium to long term for the benefit of our customers.

role_appointments.title: Abi Howarth - Director of Land Registration Services

details.body:
Forestry Commissioners have a number of specific statutory duties and powers, summarised in the Forestry Act as:

  • promoting the interests of forestry
  • the development of afforestation
  • the production and supply of timber and other forest products

In practice, these mainly relate to providing incentives (grants), regulation though controls on felling, plant health issues and managing the public forest estate.

The Commissioners have a legal duty to seek a reasonable balance between the production and supply of timber and the interests of conservation. In promoting the multiple benefits of forestry, they also seek to take careful account of people’s needs and wishes, including local communities.

The HM Land Registry Board Chair works closely with the Chief Executive and Chief Land Registrar in leading the development of the organisation. The role has responsibility for ensuring that proper governance practices are followed and performance is monitored effectively.

The Chief Operations Officer is responsible for all registration services, completing the register, customer services and client account management.

The Chief Land Registrar is responsible for keeping the land registers and has all the powers, responsibilities and duties conferred and imposed by law in relation to that.

The Chief Land Registrar is also HM Land Registry’s Chief Executive and Principal Accounting Officer. This role is responsible for the efficient and effective day-to-day management of the organisation, including overseeing its objectives and the implementation of an approved business strategy and business plan.

details.body: A Non-executive Board Member is responsible for constructively challenging, and providing guidance and support to, the Executive Board.

The Director of Finance and Business Services is responsible for the financial management of HM Land Registry and compliance with the general requirements of Government Accounting.

They oversee the preparation of our annual report and accounts and are responsible for ensuring the key performance indicator for efficiency is achieved. They provide advice to the Chief Executive and the Portfolio Board on the regularity and propriety of expenditure and also provide advice on commercial contracts.

ordered_roles.title: Director of Finance and Business Services

details.body: A Non-executive Board Member is responsible for constructively challenging, and providing guidance and support to, the Executive Board.
A Non-executive Board Member is responsible for constructively challenging, and providing guidance and support to, the Executive Board.

The Director of Legal Services is the principal legal adviser to the Chief Land Registrar and to HM Land Registry. They take administrative decisions on casework matters, including indemnity claims, and on HM Land Registry litigation.

The role is responsible for ensuring HM Land Registry has effective legal services and directs the provision of geographic information services within HM Land Registry.

The Chief Information Officer (CIO) and Director of Information Systems is accountable and responsible for the Information Systems directorate. This includes the direction of HM Land Registry’s Information Services policies, practices and resources including its personnel, external contracts, enterprise architecture and information assets.

The role is accountable for:

  • ensuring HM Land Registry’s Business Strategy remains relevant in a changing market and that all change initiatives align with and support the strategy.
  • the provision of project and programme management capabilities and governance
  • HM Land Registry’s international strategy and activities

The HR Director is responsible for setting and delivering HM Land Registry’s people strategy. This includes strategic workforce planning, leadership and management development, employee relations, wellbeing, learning and development, and ensuring colleagues have the tools and support they need to perform effectively. 

The role also supports the organisation’s strategic objectives by embedding inclusive behaviours and effective performance management across HM Land Registry.
A Non-executive Board Member is responsible for constructively challenging, and providing guidance and support to, the Executive Board.

The director is responsible for:

  • strategy, delivery and improvement of digital services
  • working with technical teams at all levels, from design through to development
  • driving the successful delivery of digital projects
  • business change, information management and assurance
  • collaborating with executive colleagues and other programme senior responsible owners (SROs)
  • supporting colleagues in driving changes to the provision of IT services

details.body: The Director of Transformation is responsible for the successful design, implementation, integration, and delivery of transformation programmes resulting from our business strategy.
In support of the Chief Land Registrar, the role has responsibility for the legal framework for land registration in England and Wales and its administrative application by HM Land Registry.

The Acting Director of Finance and Business Services is responsible for the financial management of HM Land Registry and compliance with the general requirements of Government Accounting, including the preparation of our Annual Report and Accounts.

They are responsible for strategic planning and performance, including the medium term financial plan and monitoring performance against our key performance indicators.

They are also responsible for our Internal Audit function, for providing professional procurement services to the organisation, undertaking major procurements and ensuring contracts are managed effectively, and for delivering professional and sustainable property services across our 14 offices, as well as business continuity, physical and personnel security, and health and safety policy.

The Chief Financial Officer is responsible for the financial management of HM Land Registry and compliance with the general requirements of Government Accounting.

They oversee the preparation of our annual accounts and are responsible for ensuring the key performance indicator for efficiency is achieved. They provide advice to the Chief Executive and the Portfolio Board on the regularity and propriety of expenditure and also provide advice on commercial contracts.

The Director of HR leads on all people and culture matters at HM Land Registry. They report directly to the Chief Executive and Chief Land Registrar and is a member of the Senior Executive Team. They also chair the People and Estates Committee, and attends the Remuneration and Nominations Committee.

The role involves the provision of pragmatic but creative and visionary people solutions, which have tangible business benefits, align to the Business Strategy and promote a modern and forward-thinking work culture.
